codeReview.summary: The fix addresses both prior blocking findings. FogOfWarSystem now does a full first render then incremental dirty-tile redraws without calling fogLayer.clear() on subsequent updates, and UIScene now invalidates the cached equipment stats on the previously missed equip/unequip paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The systems-worker skill says tests should import the system module directly and mock Phaser dependencies if needed, but this fog work required splitting pure logic into FogOfWarCore and using math-only tests instead of importing the Phaser-wrapped FogOfWarSystem directly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: systems-worker SKILL.md step 3 says 'Tests must: Import the system module directly'. Prior fog transcript for worker session 17b3b9ea-5914-4107-a5a2-dffe4590e61d records that importing FogOfWarSystem pulled in config.ts/Phaser symbols and led to creation of src/systems/FogOfWarCore.ts as the testable seam; the fix tests in src/__tests__/FogOfWarOptimization.test.ts still target FogOfWarCore and replicated renderer math rather than instantiating FogOfWarSystem.

summary: Reviewed mission context, prior failure reports, transcript skeleton, handoff, skill guidance, and fix commit a965ddc. Result: pass — the incremental fog renderer now consumes dirty tiles with first-render/full-render fallback, and all identified UIScene equipment mutation paths invalidate the equipment stat cache immediately.
